response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial passion (founded an open-source Geo-Data platform, FOSS4G speaker) and proven low-latency systems work handling millions of requests per second, but that systems experience is in DLang rather than modern C++ or Rust, and his only C/C++ role was brief and ended in 2015. His recent career (2020-present) is TypeScript/Node full-stack product work, which is a significant drift from the C++ routing-pipeline core of this role. Strong engineering fundamentals and adjacent domain exposure make him worth a conversation, but the primary technology mismatch and lack of navigation/routing pipeline experience are real risks.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial/GIS domain experience via a self-founded mapping platform used in production
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching at millions of req/s)
Strong engineering craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, community teaching
concerns: No recent modern C++ or Rust experience; systems work was in DLang and C++ role ended in 2015
No direct experience with routing engines, map data pipelines, or tile generation
Last five years focused on TypeScript/Node web product work, far from the role's core stack
